mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and falling corporate output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 5-day returns in >55% of observations, or the regime-specific coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, markets price in restrictive liquidity conditions and higher borrowing costs, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The specified threshold condition yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods show negative 60-day returns in >55% of instances, or the rolling correlation between payroll changes and forward returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing and inventory misalignment, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 5-day returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production alongside rising housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase signals robust capacity utilization and capital investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined supply-side expansion yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of recovery periods, or the regime-conditional alpha is not statistically significant at the 95% confidence level.
